items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s
source.population: Adults and young people aged 16 years and older with suspected transient loss of consciousness.
source.setting: Initial assessment, emergency transfer, referral and specialist evaluation of transient loss of consciousness.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected event-history, witness-account, vital-sign, cardiovascular or neurological assessment, ECG-result, urgent-risk and transfer-of-record documentation. It is international guidance and does not establish the diagnosis or UAE disposition.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2 and 1.3 — postural hypotension and specialist cardiovascular assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.

items.rationale: CG109 supports clinician follow-up of blackout or syncope events, recurrent-event history, postural context, cardiovascular assessment, and already selected investigations without assigning a cause.

items.final_wording: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.5.4 — information, safety, driving, and recurrence

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.

items.rationale: The information and recurrence section supports documenting clinician-provided information, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating driving or recurrence instructions.
